input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate to the modern configuration format after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me look at their files to understand the current setup.

data.content: `.testsettings` is a legacy format that modern MSTest (v3+) ignores. You need to migrate to a **`.runsettings`** file. Here's the equivalent configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Equivalent to the TestTimeout property (30 seconds) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout (matches the old TestTimeout property) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.reasoningText:
The user has a `.testsettings` file with:
1. Assembly resolution with `useLoadContext="true"`
2. Test timeout of 30000ms (30 seconds)
3. Code coverage data collector

They've upgraded to MSTest 3.8.0. The `.testsettings` format is legacy and no longer supported by modern MSTest. They need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file.

Let me create the equivalent `.runsettings` file and update the `.csproj` if needed.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Equivalent to the TestTimeout property (30 seconds) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout (matches the old TestTimeout property) -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces AssemblyResolution / useLoadContext -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage />
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Here's what changed and why:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000" />` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>` (per-test) and `<TestSessionTimeout>` (whole run) |
| `<TestDirectory useLoadContext="true" />` | `<DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>` — loads assemblies in-place instead of copying to a deployment dir |
| Code Coverage `DataCollector` (uri v1.0) | Updated to the v2.0 URI with `<CodeCoverage />` config block |

**To use it**, either:
- Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
- Or add `<RunSettingsFilePath>local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>` to a `<PropertyGroup>` in your `.csproj` to make it the default.

You can safely delete the old `local.testsettings` file once validated.
